title: GMS Week 18 - BLOCKADE BECOMES STRUCTURE!
summary:
Main
The Week 17 view that an indefinite ceasefire without a concrete proposal would keep owners from releasingtonnage has been overtaken by a supply shock previously seen as a tail risk. Brent traded as high as USD 126.41 per barrel on April 30, the highest since 2022, before settling into a USD 108 to USD 114 range. The June contract led the rally into expiry after reports that US Central Command may brief President Trump on renewed military action against Iran, including a "short and intense wave of strikes." Trump rejected Tehran's proposal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, confirming the US naval blockade will remain until a broader agreement is reached.
